review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Global logger initialization performs filesystem side effects during module import across core backend layers.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: A monolithic constants module couples unrelated domains and expands blast radius for small changes.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Runtime-scoping abstraction in DB layer is largely pass-through, leaving intended isolation boundary unenforced.

Despite creating a `createRuntimeScopedDbProxy`, the underlying scope-enforcement hooks are no-ops, so architectural intent exists without effective policy.
detail.suggestion: Either implement real scope enforcement in the proxy/hook functions (query/update rewriting + guarded raw access) or remove the pseudo-abstraction and document a single explicit isolation mechanism to avoid false safety assumptions.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Auth middleware stack duplicates performer resolution across modules, creating redundant DB lookups and diverging trust boundaries.

evidence: `auth.ts` loads performer and computes permissions into request context.
`roleGuard.ts` re-queries performer by `userId` and re-evaluates role membership.
`PermissionManager.requirePermission` again fetches performer and computes permissions, with a separate request interface and context model.
detail.suggestion: Introduce one canonical auth context resolver that loads performer/roles/permissions once per request, then let `requireRole` and `requirePermission` consume that normalized context without additional DB fetches.

review::.::holistic::high_level_elegance.summary: Authorization model is split between role guards and permission manager without a clear top-level ownership contract.

evidence: Role-based checks (`requireRole`) and permission/context checks (`PermissionManager.requirePermission`) are both active patterns, each with own performer-loading and error response behavior.

detail.suggestion: Define a single authorization pipeline contract (e.g., identity hydration + policy engine), keep `roleGuard` as a thin policy adapter or deprecate it in favor of permission policies, and standardize middleware composition across routes.

review::.::holistic::high_level_elegance.summary: CRM Codex route owns process execution and recovery orchestration instead of delegating to an integration service boundary.

evidence: `codex.ts` includes command spawning, timeout lifecycle, sync-retry strategy, payload parsing, and response mapping in the route module.
This route-level orchestration duplicates concerns typically owned by a service/integration layer, making HTTP handlers responsible for infra behavior.
detail.suggestion: Extract a `bdClient`/integration service (run/list/show/sync-retry + parsing + typed error mapping) and keep route handlers focused on validation + response translation.

review::.::holistic::high_level_elegance.summary: A declared API envelope exists but middleware siblings emit divergent error payload contracts.

evidence: `response.ts` defines `{ data, error }` envelope via `sendOk`/`sendError`.
`error.ts` uses `sendError`, but `auth.ts` and `roleGuard.ts` return raw `{ error: ... }` objects directly; `codex.ts` also bypasses `sendError` in favor of ad-hoc responses.
detail.suggestion: Enforce one response contract at middleware/route boundaries (prefer `sendOk`/`sendError` or an equivalent centralized responder) and migrate auth/codex handlers to it for coherent top-level API behavior.

review::.::holistic::convention_outlier:.summary: CRM route index mixes multiple URL naming conventions in one module, creating convention drift and harder endpoint predictability.

evidence: `crm/index.ts` includes `/taskTypes` (camelCase), `/project_groups` and `/project_tree` (snake_case), and `/bot-commands` (kebab-case).
Sibling route hubs (`voicebot/index.ts`, `routes/index.ts`) mostly follow lowercase path segments, so CRM appears as a style island.
detail.suggestion: Define and enforce a single route-segment convention (prefer lowercase kebab-case), then add explicit compatibility redirects/aliases only where required and tracked.

review::.::holistic::convention_outlier:.summary: Uppercase `/LLMGate` endpoint is a naming outlier in the Voicebot surface and diverges from sibling lowercase routes.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Global error middleware does not log unexpected errors and inconsistently exposes raw messages, weakening operational diagnostics and client contract consistency.

evidence: Unknown-error branch uses `sendError(res, { message }, 500)` where `message` is raw `err.message` when `err instanceof Error`.
No logging call exists in `errorMiddleware` for `AppError` or unexpected errors, so stack/context can be lost at this boundary.
detail.suggestion: Centralize error serialization: log full error context server-side, emit stable client-safe messages for 5xx responses, and include consistent structured fields (`code`, correlation/request id) across all branches.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Default 400 in `sendError` introduces implicit behavior that competes with explicit status handling in middleware.

evidence: `sendError` signature defaults `status = 400`.
`errorMiddleware` uses explicit status mapping (413/400/500), creating a mixed pattern where other callers can silently rely on 400.
detail.suggestion: Remove the default status from `sendError` (or constrain with wrappers like `sendBadRequest`, `sendInternalError`) so call sites must declare intended failure semantics.

review::.::holistic::mid_level_elegance:.summary: Session URL generation has split contract semantics (`active` env-aware vs `canonical` hardcoded), causing boundary ambiguity for integrations.

detail.suggestion: Introduce a single URL policy module with explicit modes (`public`, `canonical`, `legacyAlias`) and one source of truth consumed by route handlers and integration helpers.

review::.::holistic::mid_level_elegance:.summary: Route hubs use different mounting protocols (bare, namespaced, and root fan-in), making integration seams harder to reason about and evolve safely.

evidence: `routes/index.ts` mixes bare mounts (`authRouter`, `fundRouter`, `planFactRouter`, `uploadsRouter`) with namespaced mounts (`/finops`, `/voicebot`).
`finops/index.ts` mounts multiple feature routers on `'/'`, while `crm/index.ts` and `voicebot/index.ts` mostly mount explicit prefixes.
detail.suggestion: Standardize hub composition with explicit module prefixes and an agreed pattern for internal sub-route mounting to reduce implicit path coupling.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: No-op runtime query wrappers create indirection without enforcing any boundary contract.

evidence: `runtimeMessageQuery`/`runtimeSessionQuery` are plain `query => query` in multiple files (e.g. `messageHelpers.ts:51-54`, `transcription.ts:17-21`, `uploads.ts:38-40`, `permissions.ts:17-18`).
Call sites treat these wrappers as meaningful policy boundaries, but they currently provide no validation, scoping, or transformation.
detail.suggestion: Centralize runtime query policy in one shared utility (or remove wrappers entirely). If runtime scoping is intentionally removed, replace wrapper usage with direct queries to reduce deceptive abstraction.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Auth request contracts are redefined per route, causing parallel interface drift.

evidence: `RoleGuardRequest`, `UploadsRequest`, `VoicebotRequest`, and `PermissionsRequest` each restate similar `user`/`performer` structure with slight differences.
Permission checks then depend on local shape assumptions (`performer.telegram_id`, `user.userId`, etc.), making inter-route seam behavior non-uniform.
detail.suggestion: Create one shared authenticated request type hierarchy in middleware/types and make routes consume that contract instead of local ad-hoc interfaces.

review::.::holistic::mid_level_elegance:.summary: Route boundaries mix orchestration styles (service-first vs raw DB/procedural), increasing seam entropy.

evidence: FinOps routes delegate business logic to service functions (`createExpenseOperation`, `upsertMonthClosure`), while CRM/Voice peers execute aggregation/permission/orchestration directly in handlers.

review::.::holistic::mid_level_elegance:.summary: External side-effect orchestration lives in HTTP route modules instead of dedicated integration services.

evidence: `performerspayments.ts` directly coordinates Drive/Docs API calls, retries (`sleep`), and business computations within route handlers.

`sessions.ts` combines protocol contracts, request parsing, and deep orchestration in a single route file.

review::.::holistic::low_level_elegance:.summary: Ticket enrichment suppresses per-record exceptions, hiding data-mapping failures.

The same area depends on cross-document linking assumptions (project/group/customer), so silent drop creates nondeterministic output without diagnostics.

review::.::holistic::low_level_elegance:.summary: Repeated `String(error)` responses flatten errors and obscure branch-specific failure behavior.

This pattern leaks implementation text and prevents callers from distinguishing recoverable/expected failure classes.
detail.suggestion: Standardize route error mapping through shared middleware (`AppError` + response envelope) and avoid raw `String(error)` in handlers.

review::.::holistic::low_level_elegance:.summary: Large in-handler transformation blocks combine unrelated concerns, reducing local clarity and testability.

evidence: `dictionary.ts` composes customer/group/project normalization and tree assembly in nested loops (`83-173`) with repeated scans (`projects.filter` inside loops).

`messageHelpers.ts` has broad mutation/canonicalization routines with many branching paths in one function (`ensureMessageCanonicalTranscription`).
detail.suggestion: Split heavy blocks into small pure transforms (normalize, group, enrich, summarize) and orchestration layer; add focused unit tests for each transform boundary.

review::.::holistic::api_surface_coheren.summary: Canonical API envelope is bypassed by auth/authorization middlewares.

evidence: `response.ts` defines `ApiResponse<T>` and `sendError` with `{ data: null, error: { message, ... } }`.
`auth.ts` returns `res.status(...).json({ error: '...' })` on all failure paths (401/500), not `sendError`.
`roleGuard.ts` and `permission-manager.ts` also emit direct ad-hoc JSON error objects with inconsistent keys (`error`, `message`, `required_permissions`, `allowed_roles`).
detail.suggestion: Route all middleware failures through `sendError` (or a single typed error helper) and standardize auxiliary fields under `error.details` to keep one predictable response contract.

review::.::holistic::api_surface_coheren.summary: Request context is redefined with incompatible types across middleware layers.

evidence: `auth.ts` defines `AuthenticatedRequest.user.permissions?: string[]` and requires `user/performer/db` on the interface.
`permission-manager.ts` defines a separate `AuthenticatedRequest` where the same fields are optional and `permissions?: Permission[]`.
`roleGuard.ts` introduces another `RoleGuardRequest` variant and relies on `db?`/`user?`, forcing runtime checks for shape that should be compile-time-guaranteed.
detail.suggestion: Create one shared `RequestContext` type (auth payload + performer + db + permissions enum type) and import it in all middleware/utilities to eliminate seam ambiguity.

review::.::holistic::api_surface_coheren.summary: Equivalent session-id flows use different validation rules and failure behavior.

evidence: `checkSessionOwnership` rejects invalid IDs via `ObjectId.isValid(sessionId)` before constructing ObjectId.
`checkProjectAssignment` fetches by session using `mongoIdUtils.toObjectId(sessionId)` without `ObjectId.isValid`, so malformed input can throw.
Thrown conversion errors fall into generic 500 paths in `requirePermission` instead of a stable 4xx validation contract.
detail.suggestion: Centralize session/project id parsing in one validator and return a structured client error (e.g., `invalid_session_id`) via `AppError` + `sendError` rather than letting ObjectId constructor exceptions leak to generic 500s.

review::.::holistic::mid_level_elegance:.summary: Authorization middleware re-queries performer after auth, duplicating boundary handoff work.

evidence: `authMiddleware` already resolves performer and attaches `authReq.performer` and `authReq.user`.
`requireRole` ignores `req.performer` and performs another `db.collection(...).findOne` by `userId`.
`requirePermission` similarly re-fetches performer by `user.userId` instead of consuming canonical auth context.
detail.suggestion: Make auth middleware the single source of performer context and let downstream guards consume `req.performer` (with optional refresh flag only where strict freshness is required).

review::.::holistic::mid_level_elegance:.summary: Permission-manager mutators collapse DB errors into untyped strings, losing boundary metadata.

evidence: `addProjectAccess`/`removeProjectAccess`/`setUserProjectsAccess` catch any error and `throw new Error('Failed to ...: ...')`.
`error.ts` has `AppError(status, code, details)` for preserving structured failure semantics, but these methods do not use it.
Resulting failures degrade to generic 500 handling paths with reduced client/actionability and weaker cross-layer diagnostics.
detail.suggestion: Throw `AppError` (or a domain error type translated to `AppError`) with stable `code` and `details` from permission mutators so middleware can preserve precise API error contracts.

review::.::holistic::mid_level_elegance:.summary: Audit logging utility reinterprets request auth context instead of consuming typed middleware output.

evidence: `projecttreeaudit.ts` takes plain `Request` and casts to a bespoke shape to extract `user.userId`/`performer._id`.
Auth and permission layers already define request context interfaces, but audit utility does not reuse any of them.
This creates silent seam drift risk when auth context fields evolve (field names/type changes are not compiler-checked here).
detail.suggestion: Accept a shared authenticated request type (or a minimal typed actor-context object) in audit helpers so actor resolution is compile-time-bound to middleware contracts.

review::.::holistic::authorization_consi.summary: Sessions router relies on implicit external auth context rather than explicit per-route permission guards, creating a fragile trust boundary.

evidence: `sessions.ts` registers many routes directly (`router.post('/get'...)`, `router.post('/active_session'...)`, `router.post('/add_text'...)`, etc.) with no `PermissionManager.requirePermission(...)` wrappers in this module (e.g., lines 1797-1874, 2177+).

By contrast, `uploads.ts` consistently wires route-level permission middleware for private endpoints (`/audio`, `/upload_audio`, `/attachment`, `/upload_attachment`, `/close_session`, `/message_attachment`).
detail.suggestion: Add an explicit router-level guard or per-route permission wrappers in `sessions.ts` (matching the module’s required capability per endpoint), so authorization is enforced locally and cannot be bypassed by mount-time mistakes.

review::.::holistic::authorization_consi.summary: `/auth/list-users` exposes performer directory data without an explicit permission requirement in-handler.

evidence: `sessions.ts` line 2134 defines `router.post('/auth/list-users', async ...)` and immediately queries `VOICEBOT_COLLECTIONS.PERFORMERS` with projected `_id/name/real_name/corporate_email/role`.
No `PermissionManager.requirePermission(...)` middleware or in-handler permission check is applied before returning this directory payload.
detail.suggestion: Require a specific permission for directory enumeration (for example `VOICEBOT_SESSIONS.UPDATE` or a dedicated `VOICEBOT_USERS.READ`) and reject unauthorized callers before DB query.

review::.::holistic::authorization_consi.summary: Public attachment endpoint bypasses the session ACL checks used by private attachment endpoints.

evidence: `uploads.ts` `/message_attachment/:message_id/:attachment_index` is guarded with `PermissionManager.requirePermission(...)` and calls `checkSessionAccess` before file delivery (lines 842-875).
`uploads.ts` `/public_attachment/:session_id/:file_unique_id` has no auth middleware and no `checkSessionAccess`, serving files based only on ID pair lookup (lines 910-965).
detail.suggestion: Either enforce the same ACL as `message_attachment` (preferred) or protect `public_attachment` with signed short-lived tokens tied to session/user scope; avoid raw identifier-based public access.

review::.::holistic::mid_level_elegance:.summary: Authorization handoff is duplicated across handlers and has already drifted into inconsistent policy branches.

evidence: `sessions.ts` `sessionAccessUtils.resolve` (lines 1096-1118) encodes one policy variant (`READ_ALL`/`READ_OWN` plus PUBLIC/RESTRICTED checks).
`sessions.ts` `/send_to_crm` re-implements a narrower inline check (lines 3251-3260) instead of reusing the same utility.
`uploads.ts` `checkSessionAccess` (lines 355-377) independently duplicates similar logic, creating multiple seams that must stay in sync manually.
detail.suggestion: Extract one canonical `authorizeSessionAccess({ performer, session, action })` service used by both files and all routes; make action-level requirements explicit (`read`, `update`, `finalize`, `share`) to prevent policy drift.

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: Active CRM routes still depend on `crm/legacy` internals, so directory boundaries no longer represent ownership.

evidence: `customers.ts` imports `./legacy/projecttreeaudit.js` to log non-legacy customer operations.
`projectgroups.ts` is in `legacy/` but is actively used for core CRUD and shares the same audit primitive, so audit concern is trapped in a legacy path.
`dictionary.ts` serves current canonical CRM dictionary responses, but its peer routes remain coupled to legacy modules, keeping legacy placement on the hot path.
detail.suggestion: Stage 1: create `backend/src/services/crm/audit/projectTreeAudit.ts` and move `writeProjectTreeAuditLog` there. Stage 2: update imports in `customers.ts` and `legacy/projectgroups.ts` to the new service location. Stage 3: keep `legacy/projecttreeaudit.ts` as a thin re-export temporarily, then delete it after callers are migrated. Stage 4 validation: run `cd backend && rg "projecttreeaudit|writeProjectTreeAuditLog" src/api/routes src/services`, `cd backend && npm run build`, and route smoke checks for `/api/crm/customers/update` and `/api/crm/project_groups/update`.

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: The `crm/legacy` folder mixes unrelated domains (bot commands, payments automation, project hierarchy CRUD), creating a catch-all package.

evidence: `performerspayments.ts` (815 LOC) combines payment analytics + Google Docs/Drive generation + settings endpoints in one legacy route package.
`botcommands.ts` is a separate bot-admin concern but lives in the same legacy directory as project-structure APIs.
`projectgroups.ts` contains active project tree CRUD and customer linkage logic, not archival-only compatibility behavior.
detail.suggestion: Stage 1: define target packages by domain: `routes/crm/payments/`, `routes/crm/project-structure/`, `routes/crm/bot-admin/`. Stage 2 move order: move `performerspayments.ts` first (highest blast radius), then `projectgroups.ts`, then `botcommands.ts`; keep compatibility re-export stubs in `legacy/` during transition. Stage 3: update route mounts to new paths while preserving external URLs. Stage 4 validation: run `cd backend && rg "./legacy/(performerspayments|projectgroups|botcommands)" src/api/routes`, `cd backend && npm run build`, and endpoint smoke checks for `/api/crm/performers-payments/*`, `/api/crm/project_groups/*`, `/api/crm/bot-commands/*`.

review::.::holistic::high_level_elegance.summary: Route modules own external integration runtime logic instead of delegating to service boundaries, blurring top-level architecture roles.

evidence: `codex.ts` implements child-process command execution, timeout control, sync-retry policy, and JSON protocol parsing directly inside a route file.
`llmgate.ts` instantiates OpenAI client and implements model/response parsing policy directly at HTTP layer.
`voicebot/index.ts` mounts `llmgate` only via legacy-cased alias `/LLMGate`, reinforcing endpoint-compat pressure in route package instead of clear service-centric composition.
detail.suggestion: Stage 1: introduce service modules `services/codex/bdClient.ts` and `services/voicebot/llmGateService.ts` with typed request/response contracts. Stage 2: reduce route files to validation + auth + service call + response mapping. Stage 3: keep legacy route aliasing in index, but map both canonical and legacy paths to the same thin handler. Stage 4 validation: run `cd backend && rg "spawn\(|new OpenAI\(" src/api/routes`, `cd backend && npm run build`, and API smoke checks for `/api/crm/codex/issues`, `/api/crm/codex/issue`, `/api/voicebot/LLMGate/run_prompt`.

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: `messageHelpers.ts` is a domain mutation/normalization service stored in route package, weakening folder semantics and navigability.

evidence: `messageHelpers.ts` contains DB update flows (`ensureMessageCanonicalTranscription`, `resetCategorizationForMessage`) and normalization utilities rather than route handlers.
The same file also contains no-op shims (`runtimeMessageQuery`, `runtimeSessionQuery`) tied to migration state, increasing hidden policy in an incorrectly scoped package.
By colocating this with actual route hubs (`index.ts`) and endpoint handlers (`llmgate.ts`), `routes/voicebot/` no longer means transport-only ownership.
detail.suggestion: Stage 1: move non-HTTP helpers to `backend/src/services/voicebot/messageCanonicalization.ts` (or `domain/voicebot/messages/`). Stage 2: update all imports to service path and leave a temporary compatibility export in `routes/voicebot/messageHelpers.ts`. Stage 3: remove no-op runtime query shims or relocate them to a clearly named migration-compat module. Stage 4 validation: run `cd backend && rg "messageHelpers" src`, `cd backend && npm run build`, and replay message-edit/categorization flows that touch canonicalization logic.

review::.::holistic::high_level_elegance.summary: Endpoint surface mixes canonical and compatibility forms without a clear primary path, reducing architectural clarity.

evidence: `voicebot/index.ts` defines both flat mounts and compatibility mounts, but `llmgate` is mounted only as `/LLMGate` (capitalized legacy spelling).
`llmgate.ts` declares internal route path `/run_prompt`, making externally visible contract dependent on mixed-case parent alias.
This creates an inconsistent API surface within the same subsystem and obscures the intended long-term entrypoint.
detail.suggestion: Stage 1: define canonical mount `/llmgate` in `voicebot/index.ts` while retaining `/LLMGate` as explicit temporary alias. Stage 2: document deprecation window and add request logging tag when legacy alias is used. Stage 3: update internal consumers to canonical path, then remove alias after cutover. Stage 4 validation: run `cd backend && rg "LLMGate|llmgate" src/api/routes/voicebot`, `cd backend && npm run build`, and call both endpoints to confirm identical behavior before deprecation.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::v.summary: Voice sessions route is a monolith mixing unrelated responsibilities in one module.

evidence: `sessions.ts` defines large sets of helper utilities (taskflow locator logic, categorization mutation helpers, attachment shaping, socket emitters) and many endpoints in a single file.
Sister modules (`uploads.ts`, `permissions.ts`) already isolate adjacent concerns, but `sessions.ts` still retains cross-cutting behavior that belongs in dedicated services/adapters.
The same file owns both HTTP boundary parsing and deep domain mutation workflows, making changes in one area high-risk for unrelated API paths.
detail.suggestion: Split `sessions.ts` by concern: move normalization/formatting helpers into `services/voicebot/*`, realtime emitters into a dedicated notifier module, and group routers by capability (`session-read`, `taskflow`, `categorization`, `project-files`). Keep route files thin (validate -> call service -> map response).

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::a.summary: Attachment shaping logic is duplicated between backend API response code and frontend store.

evidence: `sessions.ts` builds `session_attachments` from messages (`buildSessionAttachments`) with Telegram-specific URI rules and sorting.
`voiceBotStore.ts` reimplements nearly the same derivation (`buildSessionAttachmentsFromMessages`) plus URI normalization and fallback generation when explicit attachments are absent.
Upload/message APIs add fields (`uri`, `url`, `direct_uri`, `file_unique_id`) that are interpreted by duplicated client/server logic, increasing drift risk when one side changes.
detail.suggestion: Define a single canonical attachment DTO at backend boundary and always return `session_attachments` precomputed from server. Move all URI derivation and Telegram path policy to backend service helpers, and make frontend store only consume normalized DTOs without reconstruction.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::i.summary: CRM UI reimplements ID lookup/normalization patterns across modules instead of shared adapters.

evidence: `CRMKanban.tsx` defines `toLookupValue`, `resolveTicketDbId`, `resolveTicketPublicId`, and route-key fallbacks inline.
`taskPageUtils.ts` defines another `toLookupValue` plus adjacent `toNonEmptyString` and identity candidate chains.
Both modules solve the same mixed-id compatibility problem independently, making behavior diverge over time (different fallback precedence and consumer-specific branches).
detail.suggestion: Introduce a shared CRM identity adapter module (e.g., `app/src/utils/crmIdentity.ts`) with canonical helpers for object-id/public-id normalization, performer identity resolution, and route-id selection; migrate kanban/task utilities to consume it.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::p.summary: Major page components combine persistence, orchestration, and rendering logic in single files.

evidence: `SessionsListPage.tsx` includes query param parsing, localStorage migration/persistence, websocket/FAB synchronization, batch actions, and table rendering in one component.
`CRMKanban.tsx` includes data normalization, status statistics orchestration, inline editing, filter state handling, and heavy table behavior in one component.
`SessionPage.tsx` mixes fetch lifecycle, paste/upload workflow, image compression helpers, and view composition.
detail.suggestion: Extract page-level orchestration hooks (`useSessionsListFilters`, `useVoiceFabSync`, `useKanbanRowIdentity`, `useSessionPasteUpload`) and keep page/components focused on rendering + event wiring. This reduces hidden coupling between state persistence and UI behavior.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::r.summary: Runtime query scoping is implemented inconsistently across voicebot modules.

evidence: Worker handlers define `runtimeQuery` using `mergeWithRuntimeFilter(...)` with identical options.
`uploads.ts` and `messageHelpers.ts` expose runtime query functions as pass-through (`query => query`).
This leaves runtime behavior policy distributed and implicit, making it difficult to reason about what code is intentionally runtime-agnostic versus accidentally bypassing shared scoping conventions.
detail.suggestion: Centralize runtime query policy into one voicebot repository utility (explicitly named for current contract, e.g., `runtimeScopedQuery` and `runtimeAgnosticQuery`) and require modules to import one of these two explicit strategies instead of local ad-hoc wrappers.

review::.::holistic::incomplete_migratio.summary: Deprecated runtime_tag isolation is still embedded in active filters and indexes.

evidence: constants.ts still defines runtime_tag index keys (`TASKS_IS_DELETED_TASK_STATUS_RUNTIME_TAG`, `WORK_HOURS_TICKET_DB_ID_RUNTIME_TAG`) and startup indexes including `runtime_tag`.
processingLoop.ts, transcribeHandler.ts, ingressHandlers.ts and voicebotWebmDedup.ts continue calling `mergeWithRuntimeFilter(..., { field: 'runtime_tag', ... })`.
sessions.ts still writes and filters by `runtime_tag` in multiple query/update paths.
detail.suggestion: Execute a full runtime_tag retirement pass: remove runtime_tag-based indexes/filters from active read/write paths, migrate remaining records if needed, and rely only on deployment/database-level isolation with one compatibility boundary during cutover.

review::.::holistic::incomplete_migratio.summary: Session close behavior is split across alias rewrites and a separate legacy handler.

evidence: sessions.ts registers POST alias `/close_session` -> `/session_done` via `registerPostAlias`.
uploads.ts still defines an independent `router.post('/close_session', ...)` mutating session state directly.

index.ts mounts both flat and legacy routers (`router.use('/', sessionsRouter)`, `router.use('/', uploadsRouter)`, plus `/sessions` and `/uploads` aliases), preserving multiple active close-entry paths.
detail.suggestion: Keep one close implementation (`/session_done`) and make any compatibility alias a thin forwarder to that code path only; remove the independent uploads close handler to avoid semantic drift.

review::.::holistic::incomplete_migratio.summary: Legacy transcription schema is still first-class alongside canonical schema.

evidence: SessionLog.tsx and TranscriptionTableRow.tsx parse both `transcription.segments` and legacy `transcription_chunks`/`transcription_text` with fallback reconstruction logic.
voicebotWebmDedup.ts and messageHelpers.ts still treat `transcription_chunks` and plain `text` as active transcription sources.
transcription.ts includes fallback text extraction from message text when transcription payload is absent.
detail.suggestion: Define and enforce one canonical transcription payload contract, run a bounded migration/backfill for historical rows, then remove `transcription_chunks`/plain-text fallback branches from UI and backend message-processing code.

review::.::holistic::ai_generated_debt::.summary: Runtime-scope helper layer contains no-op wrappers that add ceremony without behavior.

evidence: db.ts exports pass-through wrappers (`applyRuntimeScopeToAggregatePipeline`, `patchRuntimeTagIntoSetOnInsert`, `createRuntimeScopedCollectionProxy`) returning inputs unchanged.
uploads.ts defines `runtimeSessionQuery` and `runtimeMessageQuery` as identity functions despite being used as if they enforce scoping.
detail.suggestion: Either implement real scoping semantics in one shared utility and use it consistently, or remove these wrappers and call raw queries directly to reduce misleading abstraction layers.

review::.::holistic::ai_generated_debt::.summary: Participant and access-user modals duplicate control flow/UI structure instead of sharing one picker abstraction.

evidence: Both modals replicate near-identical patterns for visibility-driven fetching, search filtering, selected-item chips with remove actions, and save/cancel handlers.
Both rely on parallel state slices in sessionsUIStore with nearly identical fields (`visible`, `loading`, `selected*Ids`, `searchValue`, `sessionId`, `current*`).
detail.suggestion: Extract a shared session-member-picker modal component and a generic modal state helper in sessionsUIStore, then parameterize label/getter/save action differences.

review::.::holistic::authorization_consi.summary: FinOps domain applies auth inconsistently: fund/plan-fact endpoints are unguarded while adjacent financial endpoints require auth/admin.

evidence: `fund.ts` and `planFact.ts` expose read/write handlers without `authMiddleware`.
Sibling FinOps routes (`fxRates.ts`, `expensesOperations.ts`, `monthClosures.ts`, `employees.ts`) consistently add `authMiddleware` and often `requireAdmin`.
detail.suggestion: Apply a uniform FinOps guard policy: mount `authMiddleware` at `finops/index.ts` and explicitly declare exceptions (if any) as opt-out routes with comments/tests. Add integration tests for unauthorized access on `/fund/comments`, `/plan-fact`, and `/plan-fact/project`.

review::.::holistic::authorization_consi.summary: CRM routes show a guarding-island pattern where only selected routers enforce auth, leaving sibling mutation routes with weaker route-level protection.

evidence: `crm/reports.ts` applies `router.use(authMiddleware)` and `router.use(requireAdmin)`.
Other CRM routers in the same hub are mounted without route-level auth in assigned files, including mutation-heavy handlers and command-spawning `crm/codex.ts`.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a CRM router-level auth baseline in `crm/index.ts` (or documented per-subrouter policy object) and require explicit opt-out for public endpoints. For `codex.ts`, enforce admin permission middleware before command execution.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: API response contract is fragmented between standardized `{data,error}` envelope and multiple ad-hoc JSON formats.

evidence: Envelope helpers (`sendOk`, `sendError`) exist and are used in auth/finops/reports routes.
Most CRM and several voicebot routes return direct `res.status(...).json(...)` with incompatible shapes (`{error}`, `{result:'ok'}`, raw arrays, nested objects).
detail.suggestion: Create a migration wrapper (`ok/error` responder adapters) and migrate by router: 1) codemod simple success/error responses to helper calls, 2) add compatibility transform for legacy clients, 3) enforce via lint rule in `backend/src/api/routes/**`.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Many handlers leak raw internal error strings to clients, creating inconsistent and potentially unsafe error disclosure.

evidence: Repeated `res.status(500).json({ error: String(error) })` pattern across CRM and VoiceBot route handlers.
Other routes (for example `crm/reports.ts`) already use friendlier user-facing messages plus internal logging, proving two conflicting error strategies coexist.
detail.suggestion: Standardize on internal logging + stable external error codes/messages. Replace `String(error)` payloads with normalized `{ code, message }`, preserving diagnostics only in logs and optional trace IDs.

review::.::holistic::api_surface_coheren.summary: Same capabilities are exposed via multiple path families, making the API surface larger and less predictable.

evidence: `fund`/`planFact` routers are mounted at root and again under `/finops`, yielding duplicate public paths.
Voicebot mounts flat routes plus legacy-prefixed aliases (`/sessions`, `/uploads`, `/upload_audio`, `/audio`, `/attachment`, `/upload_attachment`).
detail.suggestion: Publish a canonical route map and migrate clients in phases: Phase 1 add deprecation headers on alias paths, Phase 2 update clients to canonical endpoints, Phase 3 remove deprecated aliases behind a version gate.

review::.::holistic::incomplete_migratio.summary: Migration shims are still first-class runtime paths with no visible retirement boundary, prolonging compatibility debt.

evidence: Legacy CRM modules remain mounted directly from the main CRM index.
Voicebot routes explicitly maintain legacy aliases and backward-compatible endpoints in the main router path.
detail.suggestion: Track a sunset matrix per alias/module (`owner`, `client usage`, `kill date`) and enforce with runtime metrics. Disable unused legacy routes first, then progressively remove legacy mounts from `crm/index.ts` and alias paths from `voicebot/index.ts`.

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: VoiceBot package organization is overloaded around giant route files, reducing navigability and ownership clarity.

evidence: Directory profile shows `backend/src/api/routes/voicebot/` at ~9088 LOC total with `sessions.ts` as a mixed-concern hub.
`sessions.ts` and `uploads.ts` both combine validation, auth checks, DB orchestration, and transport response logic in route-level files.
detail.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) create `backend/src/api/routes/voicebot/sessions/{list,get,mutate,taskflow,transcript}.ts` and `uploads/{audio,attachment,public,legacy}.ts`; 2) move pure validators/parsers to `voicebot/contracts/`; 3) move access checks to `voicebot/access/`; 4) keep `index.ts` as composition only. Move order: extract read-only handlers first, then mutation handlers, then alias wrappers. Import update/validation commands: `cd backend && rg "from './sessions|from './uploads" src/api/routes/voicebot -n`, `cd backend && npm run build`, `cd backend && npm run test -- --runInBand`.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Modern CRM modules depend directly on legacy modules, blurring architectural boundaries and increasing coupling.

evidence: `projects.ts` imports `writeProjectTreeAuditLog` from `./legacy/projecttreeaudit.ts`.
CRM index continues to wire both modern and legacy routes in a single hub, reinforcing mixed dependency directions.
detail.suggestion: Extract shared audit functionality from `crm/legacy` into a neutral `crm/shared/audit/` package, then update both modern and legacy routers to consume that shared module and remove modern->legacy imports.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: ObjectId parsing/normalization abstractions are duplicated across route files instead of being centralized at boundary helpers.

evidence: Multiple route files define near-equivalent `toObjectId` and value-normalization helpers.
Variants diverge subtly (null vs undefined behavior, accepted input forms), increasing hidden API differences.
detail.suggestion: Create one shared boundary utility module (for example `api/routes/crm/shared/idParsers.ts`) with strict typed semantics and replace local helpers incrementally, starting with `projects/customers/tickets` then legacy modules.

review::.::holistic::convention_outlier:.summary: CRM route naming and protocol conventions drift across siblings, making usage patterns inconsistent.

evidence: Path naming mixes `camelCase`, `snake_case`, and kebab-case within one hub (`taskTypes`, `project_groups`, `bot-commands`).
Some routes use standardized response helpers while sibling routes use legacy ad-hoc payloads, creating protocol-style islands in the same package.
detail.suggestion: Define and enforce one CRM API convention spec: route naming style, response envelope, and auth declaration. Add a lightweight route-lint check that fails on non-conforming path patterns and direct raw JSON responses in new CRM endpoints.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::s.summary: VoiceBot `sessions.ts` violates focused design by combining too many responsibilities in a single route module.

evidence: `sessions.ts` includes schema definitions, authorization and session resolution, cleanup side effects, taskflow mutation APIs, legacy alias wiring, and socket token preparation.
Adjacent helper modules exist but orchestration and endpoint logic still remain heavily concentrated in one file.
detail.suggestion: Split by use-case boundaries (session lifecycle, taskflow, transcript editing, summary, project files) and keep each module under a single responsibility with explicit imported collaborators.
